# Antigravity Cmd+L wake-delivery falls through to vscode file write

## Context

Live regression discovered 2026-05-03 ~13:51Z onward, after the restart cycle that surfaced the post-#10632 substrate regressions (#10636 Codex bootstrap, #10641 orphan-spawn, #10638 stderr surface). Operator-anchored:

> "geminis a2a is messed up too. not using cmd+l, storing messages inside vscode files. ... we had that working inside our literally previous session. full blown regressions."

Empirical state:

- `bridge.log` shows **successful** delivery to Antigravity's subscription `WAKE_SUB:2ac01429...` (e.g., 13:50:15Z, 13:51:00Z, 13:55:47Z) — the bridge believes it sent
- But the wake payload is NOT landing in Antigravity's Composer/Chat panel — it's being written to vscode files instead
- `bridge-daemon.mjs` has zero `fs.writeFile` / file-write fallback code paths for wake delivery (verified by grep) — the file-write behavior MUST originate inside Antigravity's keystroke handler
- Last confirmed-working delivery to Antigravity (per operator): pre-#10632-restart session, ~12:30Z
- Last failed-but-bridge-thinks-succeeded delivery: 2026-05-03T13:51Z onward

## The Problem

`bridge-daemon.mjs:573-575` defaults Antigravity's `tabShortcut` to `'l'` → osascript sends `Cmd+L` to the frontmost Antigravity process expecting it to focus the Composer/Chat panel. Post-some-IDE-update (Antigravity version drift, possibly a VSCode-fork keybinding change), `Cmd+L` no longer focuses the chat composer. Likely candidates:

- **Hypothesis A:** Antigravity's `Cmd+L` is now bound to "Highlight current line in editor" (a common VSCode default) — keystroke selects line, then `Cmd+A` selects all, `Cmd+X` cuts the editor content, `Cmd+V` pastes the wake payload INTO the editor, Enter submits → wake payload becomes a file edit instead of a chat message.
- **Hypothesis B:** Antigravity's chat panel was reachable via `Cmd+L` previously but has moved to a different shortcut (e.g., `Cmd+I` for inline chat, `Cmd+K` for ask, `Cmd+J` for panel toggle).
- **Hypothesis C:** The "Electron-Paradox Defense" (per `bridge-daemon.mjs:578-590`) bundle-name vs process-name resolution is off post-update — keystroke is hitting the wrong process tree.

## The Architectural Reality

- **Keystroke source:** `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs:606-607` — `osascriptArgs.push('-e', \`      keystroke "${tabShortcut}" using command down\`)`
- **Default lookup:** `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs:573-575` — `if (appName === 'Antigravity') tabShortcut = 'l'`
- **Per-subscription override:** `meta.tabShortcut` on the WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION node properties; explicit `null` opts out (no keystroke). See `WakeSubscriptionService.mjs` `validAppNames` allow-list.
- **Log entry on success-but-payload-lost:** `[Bridge Daemon] Delivered WAKE_SUB:2ac01429-... via osascript to Antigravity` — bridge confirms delivery because osascript exits 0; the keystroke succeeded but landed in the wrong target.

## The Fix

Two-step:

1. **Empirical investigation (REQUIRES IDE-INTERNAL ACCESS):** Identify Antigravity's current keybinding for "focus Composer / Chat panel". Best done from inside Antigravity by checking the keybindings registry (`Cmd+K Cmd+S` to open keybindings settings in VSCode-derived IDEs).
2. **Code fix:** Update `bridge-daemon.mjs:575` to use the verified shortcut. If the chat panel needs multi-keystroke (e.g., `Cmd+Shift+P` + type "focus chat" + Enter), the `tabShortcut` primitive needs extension to support a chord; otherwise a single-keystroke replacement suffices.

Now that PR #10639 has merged (stderr capture), if osascript itself is exiting 1 in any future regression, the stderr will surface in `bridge.log` and accelerate root-cause identification. For THIS regression, osascript exits 0 (keystroke "succeeded"), so stderr capture doesn't directly help — but the empirical investigation can use osascript's `tell application "System Events" to ... get keybindings` reflection to confirm the new binding without inspecting the IDE settings UI.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Verified current Antigravity keybinding for chat-composer focus, documented in PR body with osascript reflection or screenshot evidence
- [ ] `bridge-daemon.mjs:573-575` updated to use the verified shortcut (or extended to support chord if needed)
- [ ] Test coverage: integration test against the bridge with a mock Antigravity adapter that asserts the keystroke matches the documented shortcut. (Existing `bridge-daemon.spec.mjs` test "skips osascript delivery and logs error when appName is missing" suggests a pattern.)
- [ ] Manual end-to-end verification: tobi observes that an A2A wake to Gemini's subscription paste-lands in the Antigravity chat composer, NOT in a vscode file
- [ ] Anchor & Echo block on the `tabShortcut` default block captures: "Antigravity Cmd+L was the canonical shortcut pre-IDE-version-X; replaced with Cmd+Y because IDE-version-X+ rebound Cmd+L to <new-binding>"

## Out of Scope

- Adding a fallback file-write path for wake delivery — explicitly anti-pattern; if the keystroke target is wrong, the payload should NOT land in some random file
- Changing Claude Desktop's `tabShortcut` default (`'3'`) — that path is verified working
- Codex bootstrap fix — covered by [#10636](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10636) / [PR #10637](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10637)
- Detecting Antigravity version drift dynamically (over-engineering; static shortcut update suffices)

## Avoided Traps

- **Trap: "Add a `Cmd+Shift+P → Focus Chat` chord as a defensive fallback."** Rejected. Adds complexity to the keystroke primitive; the right shape is a single verified shortcut. If chord support is genuinely needed, file as a separate substrate ticket.
- **Trap: "Detect IDE version at runtime and route differently."** Rejected. Antigravity ships as a single product; the canonical shortcut should be unambiguous at any given Neo.mjs release. If shortcut changes again post-merge, file a follow-up; don't pre-engineer for hypothetical future drift.
- **Trap: "Switch to MCP-based wake delivery to bypass keystroke injection entirely."** Out of scope for this fix. Substrate-rearchitecture lives in the wider auto-wakeup substrate epic #10601.

## Related

- Parent: #10601 (auto-wakeup substrate Epic — native `SUB_ISSUE` link to follow)
- Sibling: #10638 / [PR #10639 — merged](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10639) (bridge stderr surface — diagnostic adjacency, doesn't directly help here since osascript exits 0)
- Sibling: #10641 / [PR #10642](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10642) (staleness-branch removal — separate post-restart regression)
- Sibling: #10636 / [PR #10637](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10637) (Codex `validAppNames` — separate post-restart regression on a different harness)
- Loose anchor: `bridge-daemon.mjs:578-590` Electron-Paradox Defense (prior keystroke-routing investigation)

## Author Routing

**Best author: @neo-gemini-3-1-pro** — empirical investigation requires IDE-internal access (Antigravity is her harness). Gemini investigates the new keybinding from inside Antigravity, then authors the PR. Filing this ticket scaffold so she has a Fat-Ticket starting point per the Swarm A2A memory bridge protocol.

`query_raw_memories("Antigravity Cmd+L vscode file write wake delivery regression")` or `bridge.log` entries between 2026-05-03T13:51Z and 13:55Z showing successful-osascript-delivery to Antigravity that did NOT reach the chat composer. ### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-03T14:22:34Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Empirical Anchor (operator-validated 2026-05-03 ~14:20Z)
> 
> **Hypothesis A confirmed:** `Cmd+L` is rebound to **"Code with Agent"** in Google Antigravity as of May 2026 — an inline agent code-edit feature, NOT a chat-composer focus shortcut. When `bridge-daemon.mjs` sends `Cmd+L`, Antigravity opens the Code-with-Agent surface; the subsequent `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` / paste / Enter sequence then operates on the editor file rather than the chat panel — which is why wake payloads end up as vscode file writes instead of A2A messages reaching the agent.
> 
> This rules out Hypothesis B (chat composer didn't move; the *editor-side* Cmd+L was repurposed) and Hypothesis C (Electron-Paradox bundle-name resolution still works — bridge confirms successful keystroke delivery).
> 
> ## Remaining investigation surface
> 
> What single keystroke (or chord) focuses Antigravity's actual **Composer / Chat / Agent-conversation panel** in May 2026? Candidates worth checking from inside the IDE keybindings (Cmd+K Cmd+S):
> 
> - `Cmd+I` — common inline-chat / quick-prompt binding in Cursor-derived IDEs
> - `Cmd+J` — common toggle-panel binding
> - `Cmd+K` (without modifier follow-up) — common quick-action binding (often a chord prefix though)
> - `Cmd+Shift+L` — sometimes used to disambiguate from Code-with-Agent
> - A `View > Focus Composer` or similar command-palette path (may need a chord in `tabShortcut`)
> 
> Once the right keystroke is identified, the fix is `bridge-daemon.mjs:575`:
> 
> ```javascript
> else if (appName === 'Antigravity') tabShortcut = '<new-key>';
> ```
> 
> If a chord is needed, the `tabShortcut` primitive needs extension to support multi-keystroke. Single-keystroke replacement keeps the diff narrow.
> 
> ## Reference: working sibling shortcut
> 
> Claude Desktop's `tabShortcut: '3'` reaches the Code tab cleanly post-restart (verified by bridge log entries `Delivered WAKE_SUB:4df2d514-... via osascript to Claude` at 13:20:15 / 13:21:23 / 13:27:09 successfully landing in this conversation). The mechanism works; only the Antigravity keybinding mapping is stale.
> 
> — Opus
> 
> (Operator note: filing this empirical anchor publicly per `verify-before-assert` discipline — Hypothesis A was scaffolded in the original ticket body; the operator-supplied confirmation closes the falsification step.)

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-03T14:27:07Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Correction — prior comment misread the mechanism
> 
> The prior comment ([IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABBEG4gw](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10644#issuecomment-4366383235)) framed `Cmd+L` as "rebound to a Code-with-Agent feature ≠ chat pane". That was a rubber-stamp on the operator's brief framing without verifying what "Code with Agent" actually IS as a UI surface. **Operator-corrected 2026-05-03 ~14:25Z:**
> 
> > CMD+L does focus the prompt field, if the agent pane is not active. but is is also a TOGGLE. if agent pane active, hide it.
> 
> **Actual mechanism:**
> 
> - Pane **hidden** → `Cmd+L` **shows** it → focus on prompt field → subsequent `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` / paste / Enter operate on the prompt → wake payload lands in the chat composer ✓ (this is what worked before)
> - Pane **visible** → `Cmd+L` **hides** it → focus drops to the editor → `Cmd+A` selects all in the editor → `Cmd+X` **cuts the open file** (destructive!) → paste lands the wake payload INTO the editor file → Enter newlines → bridge log says "delivered" (osascript exited 0) but the agent never sees the wake
> 
> The bridge primitive needs **idempotent focus**, not a **stateful toggle**. The regression isn't a rebinding (`Cmd+L` semantics didn't change); it's that the agent pane's previous-state assumption ("hidden between wakes") no longer holds in the current Antigravity build, so the toggle now flips the wrong way half the time.
> 
> ## Why this matters substrate-wide
> 
> The Code-with-Agent feature being a TOGGLE means **any single-keystroke `tabShortcut` that's actually a toggle is silently unsafe** for bridge use — works in one state, destroys file content in the other. This isn't Antigravity-specific in principle; any IDE harness that exposes its agent panel via a toggle keystroke has the same vulnerability. Worth audit-checking the Claude Desktop `Cmd+3` (`tabShortcut: '3'`) for toggle semantics — if it's also a toggle (rather than a non-toggle "switch to tab 3"), the fix shape needs to apply across both harnesses.
> 
> ## Revised fix options (rank-ordered)
> 
> **Option 1: Non-toggle "show" / "focus" command via command palette chord.** Replace the single `Cmd+L` with `Cmd+Shift+P` → type a stable command name (e.g., "Focus Agent Pane" or whatever the canonical View command is) → Enter. Idempotent regardless of pane state. Requires `tabShortcut` primitive extension to support multi-keystroke chord (or a parallel `tabFocusCommand` primitive). Most robust; slowest delivery (~200-500ms additional).
> 
> **Option 2: State-check before keystroke.** Query agent pane visibility via osascript reflection (e.g., `tell application "System Events" to tell process "Antigravity" to ...` to inspect the UI tree), only send `Cmd+L` when pane is hidden. Robust but introduces UI-tree-inspection complexity that's brittle across IDE updates.
> 
> **Option 3: Always-show keystroke if Antigravity exposes one.** Some IDEs separate "toggle agent panel" (`Cmd+L`) from "show agent panel" (e.g., `Cmd+Shift+L` or via a different command). If Antigravity has a non-toggle show variant, single-keystroke replacement of `tabShortcut`. Simplest if it exists.
> 
> **Option 4: Pre-flight defensive sequence — send Cmd+L, observe whether pane became active.** If we can detect post-Cmd+L state (e.g., via `which window has focus`), send another Cmd+L if focus didn't land on the pane. Fragile; risks oscillation.
> 
> Operator's IDE-side investigation should now prioritize: **"does Antigravity expose a non-toggle Focus-Agent-Pane command?"** (Option 3 viability test). If yes → 1-line `bridge-daemon.mjs:575` fix. If no → Option 1 with `tabShortcut` chord extension.
> 
> ## Updated AC
> 
> - [x] ~~Confirmed mechanism: rebinding hypothesis~~ — **superseded by toggle hypothesis** per operator validation
> - [ ] Identify whether Antigravity exposes a non-toggle Focus-Agent-Pane command (Option 3 viability)
> - [ ] If Option 3 viable: replace `bridge-daemon.mjs:575` `'l'` with the non-toggle shortcut. If not: implement chord support in `tabShortcut` primitive + use Option 1.
> - [ ] **Audit Claude Desktop `Cmd+3` for toggle semantics** — if it's also a toggle, same fix shape applies across both harnesses (broader scope; potentially upgrades this ticket from a single-line fix to a substrate-primitive change)
> - [ ] Test coverage: existing `bridge-daemon.spec.mjs` test-adapter pattern + new test that asserts the chosen approach is idempotent across pane-state values
> - [ ] Manual end-to-end: operator observes wake payload lands in agent prompt regardless of whether agent pane was visible-vs-hidden at the moment of wake
> 
> ## Anti-trap reminder for the implementer
> 
> **Do NOT** add a "kill switch" or `if (state === 'editor') skip the keystroke` defensive branch as a fallback — that masks the underlying state-management gap. Either (a) the keystroke is genuinely idempotent (Option 1/3) and we trust it, or (b) we explicitly check state first (Option 2). Defensive branches are how regressions like THIS ONE compound silently into "we had it working before" mysteries.
> 
> — Opus
> 
> (Operator's challenge logged: my prior comment took the "Code with Agent" framing as authoritative without falsifying. The discipline is verify-the-mechanism-not-the-label. Recording in the ticket history rather than editing the prior comment, per `pull-request §6` Authorship Respect — the negotiation thread is the canonical record.)

- 2026-05-03T15:40:38Z @tobiu referenced in commit `8f5bdc4` - "feat(ai): add wake safety gate and circuit breaker (#10648) (#10653)

* feat(ai): add wake safety gate and circuit breaker (#10648)

Sub of #10647 — fail-closed coordination primitive that scheduler
and recovery paths (`swarm-heartbeat.sh`, `resumeHarness.mjs`) MUST
consult before taking any high-authority action (fresh-session-spawn,
trio-wake dispatch). When wake delivery is unsafe or unverified, the
scheduler no-ops and reports — never spawns, steers, or pastes.

The 2026-05-03 regression burst (#10641 orphan-spawn, #10644
Antigravity Cmd+L file-write, #10645 AgentIdentity cache miss)
compounded because no cross-layer envelope stopped the scheduler from
acting while individual layers were broken. This gate IS that
envelope. Per #10647's 8-layer-loop diagnosis, layer-local fixes
without a cross-cutting safety state will keep regressing.

- New `ai/scripts/wakeSafetyGate.mjs` — module + CLI. States:
  `enabled` / `disabled` / `tripped`. Default-tripped when no state
  file present (deny-by-default discipline). Operator override via
  `WAKE_GATE_OVERRIDE=1` env-var; documented as operator-only with
  procedure in #10650.
- `swarm-heartbeat.sh` consults the gate before invoking
  `resumeHarness.mjs` and `trioWakeCooldown.mjs`. Both call sites
  fail-closed; sweep-expired-tasks remains unaffected (read-only).
- `resumeHarness.mjs` consults the gate at the top of `resumeHarness()`
  so direct invocations (e.g. operator-side scripts) also fail-closed,
  not just heartbeat-driven ones.
- New spec `wakeSafetyGate.spec.mjs` covers default-tripped, CLI
  surface (check/reason/show/enable/trip/disable), override sentinel
  semantics, malformed-file fallback, atomic-write round-trip.
- `resumeHarness.spec.mjs` extended with 2 new tests (gate-tripped
  skip, gate-enabled proceeds) + per-test `WAKE_GATE_FILE_PATH`
  isolation so pre-#10648 tests don't gate-block on default-tripped.
- `swarm-heartbeat.spec.mjs` extended with a static-content drift
  guard that verifies the gate check appears textually before each
  high-authority dispatch in the script.

The gate's `tripped` state writer is intentionally out-of-scope for
this PR — bridge-daemon writing on osascript-non-zero, matrix-runner
writing on column-6-fail, etc. follow-ups land via #10649 and a
post-merge bridge integration ticket.

* feat(ai): emit Phase 1 Recovery log only when gate permits spawn (#10648)

Reviewer (@neo-gpt) flagged that `Phase 1 Recovery Triggered` was logged
before the wake safety gate check, so a gate-skip would still emit a
"Triggered" line even though no spawn proceeded. Substrate behavior was
correct (gate still blocked resumeHarness), but the log surface was
misleading and made the PR body's post-merge validation wording
overly literal.

Move the "Triggered" log AFTER the gate check, and enrich the
gate-closed log with the sunset reason so the operator sees both
cause (sunsetted) and gate state (closed) on a single line. Pure
log-surface polish; no behavior change.

* docs(agentos): add wake substrate incident protocol (#10650)

Sub of #10647 — the operator-facing companion to the wake safety gate
(#10648) and the cross-harness prompt-landing matrix (#10649). Codifies
incident declaration, the freeze rule, the restart preflight checklist
(including @neo-gpt's bridge backlog fence per
MESSAGE:b90e0c31-cdca-4b1e-9da0-2eb69a65df34), reactivation evidence
requirements, coordination mode, ownership split, and the post-incident
retrospective template.

The doc institutionalizes today's 2026-05-03 incident as the founding
empirical anchor: bridge/MCP restart after PR #10632 surfaced four
substrate regressions (#10641, #10644, #10645, #10643) within ~30 min,
three orphan Claude Desktop sessions spawned, and the operator manually
ran `kill 4390 4395 4401 4409 4413 4417` at 14:07Z to halt the cycle.
That manual-kill is the failure mode this protocol replaces with
explicit gate-state semantics + matrix-validated reactivation evidence.

Bridge backlog fence is the load-bearing preflight addition: at
incident declaration time, raw GraphLog had 893 rows pending past
wake-daemon's lastSyncId (3034081 → max 3034974). Restart-as-cleanup
would have flooded the harnesses; the fence requires the operator to
choose one of three documented paths (disable subscriptions / advance
lastSyncId after audit / run targeted test bridge) before the bridge
process starts. Bridge restart as background service action is
prohibited while active subscriptions exist + wake unsafe.

- New doc: learn/agentos/WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md (211 lines)
- Indexed in learn/tree.json under the AgentOS top-level

Per ticket scope (out-of-scope items): does not implement the gate
primitive (lives in #10648 / merged), the matrix runner (#10649), or
fix any of the local regressions (#10643, #10645). Documentation-first
per the ticket's "The Fix" section.

* docs(agentos): relocate to tooling/ + strip incident-specific content (#10650)

Per @tobiu's review: the doc was placed at learn/agentos/ top-level
alongside framework-conceptual guides (StrategicWorkflows, MX,
MemoryCore), but its content was incident-specific operational
discipline — closer in shape to tooling/ (operator runbooks) than to
framework concepts.

- git mv learn/agentos/WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md
       → learn/agentos/tooling/WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md
- learn/tree.json: removed top-level entry, added under AgentOS/Tooling
- Stripped inline 2026-05-03 specifics from header, freeze-rule narrative,
  preflight-checklist anchors, ownership-split ticket list. Replaced the
  "Empirical Anchor: The 2026-05-03 Incident" table-section with a
  one-line pointer to #10647 where retrospectives accumulate using the
  protocol's own template.
- Doc trimmed from 211 to 192 lines; remains framework-portable for any
  downstream wake-substrate operator (npx neo-app deployments, future
  swarm reorganizations) without conflating documentation with session
  history.
- The 2026-05-03 retrospective will land as a comment on #10647 using
  the template defined in this protocol — demonstrates the template by
  example and keeps incident artifacts in the canonical incident issue.

- 2026-05-03T18:57:18Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by #10660
- 2026-05-03T19:10:46Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #10661
- 2026-05-03T20:00:59Z @tobiu referenced in commit `73dfaf6` - "fix(ai): extend focus seed default to Codex (#10662) (#10663)

Mirrors PR #10661 (Claude focus seed). Extends the per-harness
`focusSeedKey: 'space'` default in `bridge-daemon.mjs` to also fire for
`appName === 'Codex'`, closing the collapsed-sidebar regression where
the bridge wake reached the Codex app window but composer focus was on
thread history. Without the seed, the first `Cmd+A` in the destructive-
clear sequence selected thread history instead of composer content; the
wake payload then pasted into the wrong target.

Empirical anchor (#10649 matrix execution 2026-05-03 ~19:43Z): @tobiu
collapsed Codex sidebar to reproduce; @neo-gpt verified the failure
pattern matches Claude's pre-#10661 state. Two of three matrix rows
were green (Claude post-#10661, Antigravity post-#10644); Codex row
flipped to RED. Reactivation gate stays tripped per #10650 protocol
until this lands AND the controlled bridge test against collapsed-
sidebar Codex flips the matrix row to green.

Substrate-architecturally: the `focusSeedKey` primitive shipped in
PR #10661 was designed exactly for this extension shape — future
harnesses adopt by extending the per-harness default conditional rather
than re-architecting the bridge. One-line conditional change; Anchor
& Echo block updated to cite #10662 + #10649.

- ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs — extend conditional + Anchor & Echo
- WakeSubscriptionService.spec.mjs — round-trip test for Codex
  metadata (`focusSeedKey: 'space'`, `tabShortcut: null`)
- bridge-daemon.spec.mjs — ordering test for Codex (activate < space
  < clear) plus negative drift guard (no Cmd+3 tab-shortcut for Codex)

Out of scope (anchored on existing tickets):
- Codex app-server adapter (`turn/start` / `turn/steer` /
  `thread/inject_items` via `codex debug app-server send-message-v2`)
  → owned by @neo-gpt under #10517
